title: Where can I learn more about the Evangelion OST
display_title: Where can I learn more about the Evangelion OST
wikitext:
[]: Where can I learn more about the Evangelion OST??             Quote Post by L4plac3_ » Thu Feb 05, 2026 5:43 pm Hallo sorry I've never used forums b4 idk how 2 do this... Anyways I've been madly in love with NGE's orchestral soundtrack (the main 3 albums) in the last year, but I can never find any discussion on it, interviews from Sagisu, or even sheet music of 90% of the OST. I would love to get my hands on the directors score of this music so I can pick it apart, but I only ever find the same 6 songs over and over. I'm also a classical instrumentalist and would love to play this stuff as well, but sites like Musescore only ever have the same few songs too. I've spent the last year losing my everloving mind trying to pick apart this OST, but its extremely inaccessible unfortunately :C Do any of you have fun facts about the OST or know of somewhere I can learn more about it?? Anything would be helpful !!! Tyy

[]: Re: Where can I learn more about the Evangelion OST??             Quote Post by Darth Plato » Thu Feb 05, 2026 8:42 pm I saw a video where a guy discussed the vinyl editions of the soundtrack in detail. Stuff is out there. Here are a couple of facts I have picked up. The opening song Cruel Angel's Thesis was written by the singer and she had minimal knowledge of only the basics of the premise. There are no subtle clues about deeper meanings of the show in the song. Anno himself wrote the lyrics of komm suesser tod himself, as a poem, presumably originally in Japanese. Good luck on your quest.... I suspect some clever search queries will help in the meantime before someone more knowledgeable than me comes along.
